item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: The biggest video games set to be released this year
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: We got an early look at three of the biggest games coming to Switch 2. This is our verdict
item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies aged 43
item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.title: Why we can continue to call our cheese feta, but fontina will be a no-go
item.description: T﻿he names used in dozens of common goods will change under new rules in the recently inked Australia-European Union trade agreement.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands addresses media after exiting court in $100 million showdown with ARN
item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.title: Businesses urge government to decrease $3 billion tax to avoid 'even worse outcomes' in fuel crisis
item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.title: Apple almost went broke – now it's celebrating 50 years
item.description: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

item.title: Australia to phase out discounted junior wages for young workers
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said the landmark ruling was a "great outcome" for workers across key sectors.

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.title: RBA to end card surcharges as cash usage dwindles
item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.title: 'Doesn't pass the pub test': Hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges
item.description: Inside the hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges.

item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.title: New COVID strain circulating in Australia as it spreads across the globe
item.description: Experts say there are no signs the variant BA.3.2, otherwise known as "Cicada", poses a bigger health threat than other variations.

item.description: "The more I looked at it, the more I discovered red flags."

item.description: A﻿ustralian tech giant Canva has snapped up two local artificial intelligence (AI) companies. But bigger news is on the way.

item.description: T﻿heir bottom line will be impacted by any prolonged disruption of aviation fuel, analysts warn.

item.description: T﻿he CEO admitted "the roles people play are shifting".

item.description: Payslip complacency has reached remarkable highs.

item.description: Virgin Australia has joined Qantas in cutting flights and hiking ticket prices as skyrocketing fuel prices hit local airlines.

item.description: F﻿ollowing years of rumours, Apple may be about to deliver a missing link in its iPhone range.

item.description: A﻿ nation-wide study of our wastewater and online search data has revealed the worrying trend is booming.

item.description: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.

item.description: The government is set to slash the cost of running the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and aged care under sweeping new changes.

item.description: T﻿he inquiry has been marked by scuffles between investigators and delivery service employees, a feigned medical emergency and hastily scribbled notes to "stay silent".

item.description: They seem harmless upon first glance, but look a little closer and you'll see the truth.

item.description: Many critics have sounded the alarm on joining two of Hollywood's biggest remaining five studios.

item.description: People used to shame it, but clearly something is working for Aussie shoppers.

item.description: Anyone who has been to these locations recently should monitor for symptoms, authorities say.

item.description: Woolworths has seen a jump in grocery sales but doesn't expect it to last, slashing its profit forecasts amid high petrol prices.

item.description: The Reserve Bank's third successive rate hike hit mortgage holders like a kick in the guts and there is little light to be found at the end of the tunnel when digging deeper.

item.description: It's the worst remuneration strike in recent Australian corporate history in a stunning blow to the embattled network.

item.description: The carpark construction, which would have created an additional 296 parking spots, would have come at the expense of the Hyper Karting track, axing more than 150 jobs in the process.

item.title: Small businesses and tradies to permanently keep $20,000 tax break in federal budget
item.description: The $20,000 threshold will apply per asset and will expand across tools, technology or other assets, both new and second-hand, and can be claimed in full at the end of every financial year.﻿

item.description: Anthony Albanese said "what does a royal commission do besides fund lawyers?"

item.description: The federal opposition has opened the door to a potential partnership with One Nation in an attempt to unseat the Labor government. ﻿

item.title: Business owners mock Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's CGT changes with AI meme trend
item.description: Anthony Albanese is being mock-hailed as a 47 per cent "partner" by business owners online in a wave of AI memes.

item.description: Last week's changes to the ﻿capital gains tax discount and negative gearing will likely shift people away from rentvesting.

item.description: "That's going to affect us badly."

item.description: Forty Australian business owners under 40 have penned an open letter to the prime minister.

item.description: The opposition has signalled plans to protect cash as a payment method in legislation as part of their pro-small business campaign.

item.description: It has been almost three weeks since the five-year-old girl was found dead in Alice Springs.

item.description: The ex-partner of former Labor leader Mark Latham might have dropped her application for court protection but some grievances remain in the public domain.

item.title: Weapons expo to return to Melbourne, despite last one sparking mass protests
item.description: A controversial weapons exposition will return to Melbourne in two years' time, despite the last event sparking some of the city's largest protests in decades.

item.title: 'They've got a real China problem': US senators question Trump's pick for ambassador to Australia
item.description: US President Donald Trump's pick for ambassador to Australia has pledged to focus on AUKUS, critical minerals, antisemitism and China's growing influence in the region.

item.description: T﻿he former prime minister has criticised wealthy people's "howls" about the current government's tax reform. ﻿

item.description: T﻿here have been more than 230 cases in most states and territories.

item.description: The number of unemployed Aussies has soared by 33,000 in the past month.

item.description: T﻿he Queen was "very keen" to see her son take on a "prominent role", newly released documents have revealed.

item.description: A customs spokesperson said the passenger "should not have boarded" at all.

item.title: Opposition leader backs Jacinta Price after questions about migration remark
item.description: Jacinta Price denied agreeing to a remark that Australia needs to stop "flooding" itself with migrants from India, China, Africa and the Middle East.

item.title: Government 'unaware' of booked flights for ISIS brides returning home
item.description: Six women, their children and grandchildren have reportedly left a Syrian camp for Australia.

item.description: G﻿uzman y Gomez has scuttled its plans for an ambitious expansion in the United States and will immediately shut its existing stores in Chicago.

item.description: T﻿he One Nation leader made the remark just seconds after her adviser said 'shut up' to a journalist.

item.title: PM refuses to rule out changes to the 'death tax'
item.description: Labor has been fielding questions about the so-called death tax during its nationwide campaign to promote the budget, which it has dismissed as a Coalition scare campaign.

item.title: Victorian police officer dies after falling during hike on Inca Trail in Peru
item.description: Matt Paton has been remembered by his colleagues as someone who loved "travelling, exploring historic sites and cultures".

item.title: US says 'slight progress' in Iran talks amid uncertainty about whether war will resume
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says there was "slight progress" during talks with Iran amid uncertainty about whether a deal will be reached or war will resume.

item.title: Today in History - May 23: Child actor had no idea he was starring in a horror movie
item.description: The classic horror film The Shining, starring Jack Nicholson and directed by Stanley Kubrick, was released to American cinemas on May 23, 1980.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as director of national intelligence, citing her husband's health
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard has resigned as President Donald Trump's director of national intelligence.

item.title: Donald Trump confirms he won't attend his son's wedding, claims to be too busy
item.description: Donald Trump has announced he won't attend his son's wedding, saying he is too busy.

item.title: Pentagon releases second batch of declassified UFO files following previous release of more than 160 files
item.description: P﻿olls have found that most US citizens believe aliens exist, and the latest batch of files from the Pentagon has done little to dispel the theories.

item.title: Trump posts AI video of himself attacking Stephen Colbert
item.description: Donald Trump has posted AI-generated video of himself throwing Stephen Colbert into a dumpster.

item.title: Congo curtails funeral wakes in Ebola outbreak as WHO upgrades risk assessment
item.description: Authorities in north eastern Congo banned funeral wakes and gatherings of more than 50 people on Friday.

item.title: One Nation would form federal opposition, Labor re-elected, according to new polling
item.description: Federal Labor would be returned to power, the Coalition wiped from the map in four states and One Nation the official opposition party.

item.title: At least 90 killed in China coal mine gas explosion, dozens trapped
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people, state media reported Saturday and after dozens were said to be trapped underground.

item.title: Fears leaking chemical tank will explode, 40,000 told to evacuate in Southern California
item.description: "This thing is going to fail, and we don't know when."

item.description: A notorious vigilante from Brazil who hunted down serial killers and other criminals, had his first taste of jail on May 24, 1973 at the age of 18.

item.title: Minns admits 'giant intelligence failure' led to Bondi terror attack

item.title: Vivid Sydney cancels shows after drones plummet from sky due to 'technical difficulties'
item.description: This year is a comeback for the drone show, which proved so wildly popular in 2024 that it was cancelled last year due to safety concerns.

item.description: T﻿he group are believed to have entered the cave seeking gold before a flash flood blocked the exit.

item.title: Four dead, five injured in crash between train and school bus in Belgium
item.description: The train was estimated to be travelling at about 120km/h as it approached the crossing and had "no time to brake".

item.title: 'No relation to reality': Son suspected of killing billionaire dad steps down, protests innocence
item.description: Jonathan Andic, 45, was arrested in Barcelona last week, 17 months after his father, Mango founder Isak Andic, fell more than 100 metres to his death.

item.title: 'Far better off': Australian bosses warn of mass exodus to NZ and USA after sweeping tax changes
item.description: NZ finance minister Nicola Willis may have been tongue-in-cheek when she invited Australians to move across the ditch, but it appears many business owners are seriously considering the move.

item.title: Today in History - May 27: Out-of-control fire under small town expected to burn for centuries
item.description: After years as an underground trash dump, a disused mine shaft in Centralia, Pennsylvania had become a foul-smelling haven for vermin.

item.description: T﻿he 79-year-old president declared doctors gave him the all-clear despite concerns about his advancing age.

item.description: P﻿assengers are being urged to check with their airlines through today.

item.description: S﻿he says the changes will make it harder for a whole generation to gain a foothold on the property ladder.

item.description: This week's wet weather is only set to get worse.

item.title: NASA unveils plans to send humans back to the moon before Trump's presidency ends in 2028
item.description: The space agency released details of a three-phase moon mission that will eventually build habitable infrastructure o the moon to allow humans to "live and operate" on the lunar service.

item.description: Republicans are fretting that a candidate endorsed by Donald Trump may cost them in their most important state.

item.description: The 41-year-old made his debut on the rich list with a staggering personal fortune amassed from an AI company.

item.title: Westpac cops $26 million penalty over 'grossly negligent' hardship failures
item.description: The Federal Court has ordered Westpac Banking Corporation pay a $26 million civil penalty after leaving vulnerable customers facing financial hardship in the lurch.

item.description: A﻿n investigation has been launched.

item.title: Labor MP to be investigated over suspected electoral breach
item.description: S﻿he made Australian history at the last election but now she will be investigated over an alleged electoral breach.

item.title: Double killer jailed for longer after 'inadequate' 14-year sentence
item.description: Four years were added to his sentence after the killing of two sex workers in 2022.

item.title: 'Lethal kinetic strike' on smuggling boat in 'narco-trafficking route'
item.description: The elaborate rigging is far bigger than the building itself.

item.title: JobSeeker to be overhauled under once-in-a-generation changes
item.description: It will undergo a major shake-up in the biggest proposed changes to employment services in decades.

item.title: ABC news director Justin Stevens resigns after 19 years with broadcaster
item.description: The veteran journalist made the bombshell decision due to "professional and personal" reasons.

item.title: Two men arrested over investigation into fugitive Dezi Freeman are released without charge
item.description: A 48-year-old man and a 45-year-old man, who were arrested at different locations in north-east Victoria, were released pending further inquiries today, Victoria Police said.

item.title: Aussies likely to get momentary interest rate reprieve after surprise inflation read
item.description: Better-than-expected figures could spare rate-hike weary borrowers another blow - but it could be brief.

item.title: Father of 'ISIS bride' says she made a 'mistake' but shouldn't face charges
item.description: "When you are young, you do so many ﻿mistakes, you don't get charged [for] those mistakes."

item.title: Man found guilty of murder decades after being spurned by love interest in love triangle murder
item.description: Gofal Baziad was on trial for the alleged murder of Jason Palmer after their mutual love interest chose her side.

item.title: Police say innocent family likely 'not the intended targets' of south-west Sydney shooting and firebombing
item.description: The home owner said her family, which had recently moved into the home, was asleep when multiple shots rang out.

item.title: Creek beds, waterways focus of new Gus Lamont search
item.description: The search is set to take advantage of the recent heavy rain, which may help in the search for clues into his disappearance.

item.title: Three brothers reel in catch of a lifetime off Victoria's Surf Coast
item.description: It took two hours to wrangle a fish so massive even the charter boat captain was shocked.

item.title: Five villagers missing in flooded Laos cave for more than a week found alive, rescuers say
item.description: A video posted by a Thai rescue group involved in the mission appeared to show the moment divers emerged from the water and discovered the trapped villagers.

item.title: Iran says draft deal with US would reopen Hormuz shipping, end naval blockade
item.description: Iran's state TV says it has obtained a draft of an initial, unofficial framework for a memorandum of understanding with the US on ending their conflict.

item.title: Australia's top 10 richest people revealed
item.description: As ordinary Australians have battled cost-of-living pressures over the past year, some of the country's richest people have grown their substantial fortunes.

item.title: Today in History - May 28: Parents made deal to save their quintuplets, but it cost them custody
item.description: The first quintuplets to survive beyond infancy were born in a small Canadian town on May 28, 1934.

item.title: NSW residents warned of flood risk as heavy rain and storms lash state
item.description: C﻿ommunities across the Mid North Coast are impacted to bear the brunt of the deluge in coming hours.

item.title: Bridget McKenzie insists she didn't misuse taxpayer money to attend son's wedding
item.description: T﻿he Coalition front bencher insists she paid her own way when attending the family event in Tasmania three years ago.

item.title: Queensland man charged with murder after allegedly killing man in random rampage
item.description: The series of alleged violent attacks took place over about eight minutes in the small town

item.title: Nine-months' pregnant nursery owner chases down plant thief
item.description: She managed to recover plants worth hundreds of dollars.

item.title: Jill Biden thought Joe Biden was having a stroke during debate
item.description: Joe Biden's wife feared he was having a stroke during his disastrous presidential debate with Donald Trump in 2024.

item.title: Man seemingly caught asleep while driving Tesla on major Sydney motorway
item.description: T﻿he man was seen with his eyes closed with his hands not on the steering wheel.

item.title: Trump plan would stop Australians flying to most popular US destinations
item.description: Australians would not be able to fly into the most popular entry points to the US under a plan proposed by the Trump administration.

item.title: Almost 500,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine war, says spymaster
item.description: T﻿he estimated staggering losses are equivalent to the population of an Australian territory.

item.title: Melbourne woman charged with terrorism offences after returning from Syria
item.description: The 34-year-old woman, ﻿who returned from Syria in September 2025, was arrested at a home in Broadmeadows in Melbourne's north today and has been charged with entering and remaining in a declared conflict zone and joining ISIS.

item.title: Notorious pedophile Ashley Paul Griffith fights 'excessive' life sentence
item.description: One of Australia's worst pedophiles, childcare worker Ashley Paul Griffith, has launched a bid to slash the life sentence he received for abusing dozens of young children.

item.title: Man who attacked Lauren Huxley denied parole
item.description: The attacker behind one of the most horrific crimes in modern Australian history faced a parole hearing.

item.title: Trump's Justice Department launches criminal investigation into the woman a jury found he raped
item.description: He owes her $115 million after a jury found he sexually assaulted her. But she's the one in hot water.

item.title: 'You ugly f---': US election quickly turns nasty as White House weighs in
item.description: The centrepiece race of the US midterm elections has rapidly turned nasty, with one of the most senior staffers of the White House getting in a profane spat with Democrats online.

item.title: Chilling truth behind Trump's 'blow 'em up' threat
item.description: Every nation who thinks they are in the president's good books might want to think again.

item.title: Entertainment news headlines - 9News
item.description: Breaking entertainment news including celebrity gossip, film and movie news, music news, award ceremonies, scandals, photos and videos

item.title: Latest technology news headlines, updates & new tech innovation | 9News
item.description: Latest technology news stories including consumer electronics, science and space technology, gadgets, internet tech, medical devices, websites and social tech, cryptocurrencies and other technology news headlines

item.title: Latest health news top stories & analysis
item.description: Latest health news, research and results, scientific discoveries, diets, obesity, cancer, medical procedures and medicine, hospitals, and government health guidelines

item.title: Business news headlines - 9News
item.description: Latest business news, company tax, government regulations, CEOs, entrepreneurs, customers, and big business strategy

item.title: Politics News - 9News - Latest political news today Australia and World
item.description: Latest political news from Australia and around the world, covering elections, by-elections, our regular leadership spills, poll results, Question Time debates and daily Australian party politics

item.title: World news headlines and international current affairs - 9News
item.description: Latest world news headlines: International breaking news and current affairs from US, Europe, Middle East, Asia, Africa and more. If it happens around the world, you will know about it on 9News

item.title: News Australia - Latest news headlines in Australia
item.description: Today's news in major cities, regional and local areas which can include accident reports, police & emergency responses, criminal and court proceedings or live breaking news

item.title: No evidence to suggest Gus Lamont was abducted, as one grandparent remains a suspect
item.description: Authorities have said that while they were met with hundreds of tips from the public, no further progress has been made in finding evidence into the four-year-old's disappearance.

item.title: Accused childcare paedophile Joshua Dale Brown pleads not guilty to over 150 charges
item.description: Childcare worker Joshua Dale Brown has pleaded not guilty to abusing young children in his care and creating child abuse material.

item.title: 'Break the law, lose the bike': Public patience with e-bikes has run out
item.description: The crackdown e-bike riders have been warned about is coming after a surge in crashes and tragic deaths.

item.title: Calls for an early election over controversial tax reform
item.description: W﻿elcome to our rolling coverage on everything federal politics on Thursday, May 28. Treasurer Jim Chalmers...

item.title: Quarantine extended for Australian passengers of hantavirus cruise ship
item.description: The group will remain in quarantine at the Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience, located north of Perth, for another 21 days.
